Meta.title: Sentinello — early-warning CVE monitoring for your whole Node.js portfolio
Meta.description: Self-hosted, open-source dependency-vulnerability monitoring for Node.js projects. Point it at your repositories and get one triage queue for known CVEs across every project.
Meta.tagline: CVE early-warning for your whole Node.js portfolio

Hero.title: An early-warning system for the dependencies you stopped watching.
Hero.subtitle: In the AI age you ship more Node.js projects than you can maintain. Sentinello watches every one and surfaces known CVEs in their npm dependencies — so a forgotten project never becomes an incident.
Hero.trust: No account. No SaaS. No telemetry. One Docker image and one SQLite file — your code and your findings never leave your machine.

Why.title: Why we built this
Why.lead: In the AI age, you ship more than you can maintain.
Why.body1: A single developer now spins up, delivers, and moves on from a dozen projects a year. The marketing site, the client dashboard, the side project that quietly went to production — they keep running long after anyone last looked at them.
Why.callout: A single forgotten dependency with a critical remote-code-execution flaw is all it takes. The simplest site can become the way in.
Why.body2: Sentinello is the early-warning system for that long tail. It watches every project you point it at, so a new CVE in something you shipped months ago surfaces in one place — before it becomes a disaster.

How.title: How it works
How.subtitle: Three steps. No agents to install in your projects, no accounts to create.
How.step1Body: Mount your repositories under /roots, or add them from Settings → Roots. Every directory is auto-registered and discovered on startup.
How.step2Body: A background worker checks your dependencies against known CVEs on a schedule, installing the Node version each project pins when it needs to.
How.step3Body: Every finding across every project lands in a single queue you can filter by severity — with optional alerts to Slack, Telegram, or a webhook.

Features.title: Features
Features.subtitle: Everything in one self-hosted portal — no external services, no data leaving your network.
Features.queueBody: See and triage CVEs across your whole portfolio in one place — instead of npm audit scattered across a dozen checkouts.
Features.byProjectBody: Drill into any repository to see its current findings, fix versions, scan history, and mutes.
Features.byLibraryBody: Pivot to a vulnerable package and see every project it affects — then mute it everywhere at once.
Features.exportBody: Export findings for a project or library as Markdown, with a customizable remediation prompt for your team or an LLM.
Features.scanningBody: A background worker rescans on a schedule, so new advisories show up without you remembering to check.
Features.severityBody: Sort and filter findings by severity to handle the criticals first and the noise later.
Features.notificationsBody: Get failure and finding alerts via Slack, Telegram, or a plain webhook — in the language you choose.
Features.selfContainedBody: One Docker image and one SQLite file. No database server, no message queue, no cloud dependency.
Features.autoRootsBody: Anything mounted under /roots is registered and scanned on boot — the directory name becomes its label.
Features.nodeVersionsBody: Respects each project's .nvmrc, installing and caching the Node version it pins, once.
Features.languagesBody: The portal UI, scan reason codes, and statuses are localized to 10 languages.

SelfHost.title: Self-host
SelfHost.subtitle: Self-host in minutes — copy, paste, open localhost. Pin a version tag for production.
SelfHost.rootsNote: Anything mounted under /roots is auto-registered on startup — the directory name becomes its label — so discovery and scanning begin on their own. Mounting is optional; you can also add roots from the portal.

Screenshots.title: Screenshots
Screenshots.subtitle: See it in action — the portal scanning a handful of demo projects. Click any shot to expand it.
Screenshots.dashboardCaption: One dashboard for every project and vulnerable library across your portfolio.
Screenshots.projectCaption: Project view: current findings, fix versions, and full scan history.
Screenshots.libraryCaption: Library view: every project a vulnerable package affects, in one place.
Screenshots.exportCaption: Export a remediation-ready advisory as Markdown for your team or an LLM.

Roadmap.title: Roadmap
Roadmap.subtitle: Sentinello watches your Node.js dependencies today. Here's where it's headed — and what you can ask for.
Roadmap.sourcesBody: Beyond the npm advisory database — additional feeds like OSV, so findings are broader and land earlier.
Roadmap.integrationsBody: More notification channels and ways to plug Sentinello into the tools your team already uses.
Roadmap.sastBody: Catch risky patterns in your own source code, not just known CVEs in your dependencies.
Roadmap.secretsBody: Flag committed secrets and license issues across the same portfolio, in the same queue.
Roadmap.ctaNote: Tell us what you'd integrate or scan next — open an issue on GitHub and help shape the roadmap.

WhoFor.title: Who it's for
WhoFor.lead: Sentinello is for everyone who has more in production than they have eyes on — the solo developer, the small team, the agency juggling client work.
WhoFor.point1: You ship side projects and client sites that still need to stay safe long after launch.
WhoFor.point2: You want a portfolio-wide view without wiring CI into every repository.
WhoFor.point3: You'd rather self-host than hand your code inventory to a SaaS.
WhoFor.honest: If you're a large org with Snyk or Dependabot already wired into a mature pipeline, keep them — Sentinello isn't trying to replace enterprise SCA. It's here for the rest of your portfolio that nobody is watching. It's open source and MIT-licensed, so you can read exactly what it does.

Notifications.title: Notifications & Webhooks
Notifications.subtitle: Get told the moment a project picks up a vulnerability — and trigger your own automation to fix it.
Notifications.slackBody: Post findings to any channel via an incoming webhook, with native bold and bullet formatting.
Notifications.telegramBody: Send alerts to a chat or group through a bot — rendered with proper formatting, not raw markup.
Notifications.webhookBody: POST to any endpoint so you can route alerts wherever your team already works.
Notifications.flavorsSubtitle: Pick the payload that fits what you're building.
Notifications.jsonBody: Root, project and a vulnerabilities list — each with library, version, recommended version and advisory. Ready for an auto-fix agent to act on.
Notifications.textBody: The same advisory export shown in the portal, as text — drop it straight into an LLM to triage and fix.
Notifications.scopeBody: Send everything to one place, or wire a specific root — or a single project — to its own channel or webhook.
